title: paracorokia carpodetoides
intro: Paracorokia carpodetoides, also known as the "Ngaio", is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. It is a small tree native to New Zealand, and its bark, leaves, and roots are used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Paracorokia carpodetoides include treating skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as reducing fever and alleviating pain. In herbal preparations, the Ngaio tree is often used in infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, making it a popular natural remedy among traditional healers.
